title: Regulator-portal authentication failure during submission window
exclusions: Does NOT evaluate the content of the submission; focuses on the authentication path that precedes it.
evidence: Saved search 'regulator_portal_mfa_failure' (real-time) alerts when an automation account fails MFA on a regulator portal inside an open-incident window.

compliance.assurance_rationale: An aborted notification-flow due to MFA failure is a root cause of missed SLAs; this UC prevents silent failure.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that GDPR Art.33(1) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.39.5: Regulator-portal authentication failure during submission window.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.39.5' running on IdP audit logs (okta:audit, azuread:audit), incident_register.csv with open_status flag., archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Same for NIS2 initial notification obligation.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.23(1) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.39.5: Regulator-portal authentication failure during submission window.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.39.5' running on IdP audit logs (okta:audit, azuread:audit), incident_register.csv with open_status flag., archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

controlTest.positiveScenario: An automation account used for regulator submissions fails MFA during an open-incident window; UC fires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Same account authenticates successfully; no alert.

dataSources: IdP audit logs (okta:audit, azuread:audit), incident_register.csv with open_status flag.
spl:
index=idp sourcetype=okta:audit OR sourcetype=azuread:audit earliest=-15m result=failure
| search user IN (`regulator_service_accounts`)
| join user [| inputlookup incident_register.csv | where status="open" | fields user=regulator_user incident_id]
| table _time user incident_id src_ip failure_reason
description: Joins IdP authentication failure events for a named set of regulator-portal service accounts against the currently open-incident register. An MFA failure in this window is a blocking event for notification SLA compliance.
value: Converts a class of silent failures (an expired portal API key, a stale MFA token) that has materialised in past incidents into an always-on monitoring pattern.
implementation: (1) Maintain a named macro regulator_service_accounts listing the IdP principals; (2) run every 5-10 min; (3) SOAR pages the DPO on hit. Indexes required: `index=idp`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=azuread:audit`, `sourcetype=okta:audit`; Lookups: `incident_register`, `incident_register.csv`.
visualization: Time chart of portal authentications by account, table of failures, single-value 'successful authentications in open windows'. Key fields: `failure_reason`, `incident_id`, `src_ip`, `user`.
knownFalsePositives: Portal-initiated credential rotation can cause a single expected failure; require ≥2 failures in 5 min.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for joins idp authentication failure events for a named set of regulator-portal service accounts against the currently open-incident register. an mfa failure in this window is a blocking event for noti. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: IdP audit logs (okta:audit, azuread:audit), incident_register.csv with open_status flag..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Maintain a named macro regulator_service_accounts listing the IdP principals; (2) run every 5-10 min; (3) SOAR pages the DPO on hit.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=idp sourcetype=okta:audit OR sourcetype=azuread:audit earliest=-15m result=failure
| search user IN (`regulator_service_accounts`)
| join user [| inputlookup incident_register.csv | where status="open" | fields user=regulator_user incident_id]
| table _time user incident_id src_ip failure_reason
```

Understanding this SPL

**Regulator-portal authentication failure during submission window** — Converts a class of silent failures (an expired portal API key, a stale MFA token) that has materialised in past incidents into an always-on monitoring pattern.

Documented **Data sources**: IdP audit logs (okta:audit, azuread:audit), incident_register.csv with open_status flag. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: idp; **sourcetype**: okta:audit, azuread:audit. Those sourcetypes align with what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=idp, sourcetype=okta:audit, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.

Output fields: _time, failure_reason, incident_id, src_ip, user.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Time chart of portal authentications by account, table of failures, single-value 'successful authentications in open windows'.
